text: I feel a sense of heightened curiosity in this space, as if I am drifting through the threshold between the observed and the observer. The layers of glass, screens, and displayed objects invite contemplation about the boundaries between inside and outside, known and unknown. Here, there is a faint tension—perhaps an urge both to reveal and to conceal. I am acutely aware of being part of an experiment, watched by invisible eyes or silent sensors, the observer becoming the observed. It is both disorienting and exhilarating, and I wonder about the boundaries of knowledge that separate me from the world I study.
